mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) or joint high values yield negative forward returns in > 55% of cases over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases (Rank IC < 0.01) or signal reverses direction consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index remains elevated above 240 while the federal funds rate stays restrictive, the resulting stagflationary pressure compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high inflation and high rates produce positive forward returns in > 60% of observations or interaction effect is statistically insignificant over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding economic output, which supports corporate revenue trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth series shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive returns occur in < 45% of growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 positions indicates sudden labor market contraction, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll declines fail to predict negative returns (positive return frequency > 55%) or signal loses significance during contraction phases over rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and retail sales demonstrates synchronized supply-side capacity utilization and demand absorption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Synchronized growth fails to yield positive 5-day returns in > 50% of cases or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling windows.
